title: Sweet Birds, I Come
content:
The bird that now
     On bush and tree,
    Near leaves so green
     Looks down to see
    Flowers looking up, 
     He either sings
    In ecstasy
     Or claps his wings.

    Why should I slave
     For finer dress
    Or ornaments;
     Will flowers smile less
    For rags than silk?
     Are birds less dumb
    For tramp than squire?
     Sweet birds, I come.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Henry Davies delivers a powerful performance in "Sweet Birds, I Come"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
